name: cable rear delt row (stirrups)
instructions: Attach a stirrup handle to a low cable pulley and stand facing the machine.
Grasp the handle with your left hand and take a step back with your right foot, positioning your body at a slight angle.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
With your left arm extended and your palm facing down, pull the handle towards your chest by retracting your shoulder blade.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your shoulder blade.
Slowly release the handle back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat the exercise with your right arm.

Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged. With your left arm extended and your palm facing down, pull the handle towards your chest by retracting your shoulder blade. Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your shoulder blade. Slowly release the handle back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Switch sides and repeat the exercise with your right arm.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
